message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

[initprompt] workstream-2d-claim-a-complete

Read ui-audit/06-claim-a-plan.md, ui-audit/06a-plan-checkin.md, ui-audit/06b-claim-a-tokens-checkin.md, and ui-audit/06c-claim-a-drift-checkin.md. Read ui-audit/02-comparison.md and ui-audit/04-recommendations.md.

Goal: declare Claim A workstream complete by updating the audit artifacts to reflect what landed, what was deferred, and what was learned.

Part one, comparison file updates. For every entry in 02-comparison.md that was addressed during Claim A work, update the relevant cells to reflect the post-fix state. Add a status note with date in the existing notes field. Do not delete the original audit findings; the historical record matters.

Part two, recommendations file updates. For every Claim A recommendation in 04-recommendations.md, mark its status: implemented, partially implemented with remainder noted, deferred with reason, or rejected with reason. Recommendations rejected during execution are valuable signal for future audits.

Part three, lessons learned. Append a section to 06-claim-a-plan.md called "lessons-for-claim-b" capturing anything from Claim A execution that should inform Claim B. Examples: assumptions in the recommendations that turned out wrong, scope items that took longer than expected, multi-module implications that surprised you, decisions that should be made differently next time.

Part four, implications captured. Update or create ui-audit/07-future-work-notes.md with two subsections. First, "implications-for-claim-c-revisit" capturing any shell decisions made during Claim A that affect how the canvas seam is eventually unified. Second, "implications-for-module-rebuild" capturing any shell conventions established during Claim A that the eight rebuilt modules will inherit.

Do not modify the per-module audit files (01-console.md, 01-designer.md, 01-settings.md); they remain historical scaffolding per the original reconciliation log.

When done print: count of comparison rows updated, count of recommendation entries marked, count of lessons captured, and confirmation that 07-future-work-notes.md exists with the two subsections. Stop.

## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

attachment.content:
- audit: Verify a spec unit's implementation against its authoritative spec. Reads the manifest, reads spec files, walks target code, catalogs every gap, and writes task files for each finding.
- spec: Read the implementation spec for a given I/O module. Usage: /spec <module>. Modules: console, process, designer, graphics, display-elements, shapes, opc
- design-qa: Inventory all implementations of a cross-cutting feature across the codebase, surface inconsistencies, ask targeted design questions, and write the answers as an authoritative decision file. Run this BEFORE /audit on any feature marked ⚠️ NOT SPECCED in the manifest.
- spec-index: Print a quick-reference index of every audit unit — unit ID, canonical name, wave, and current task status. Use this to look up the correct unit ID or module name before running spec-scout or feature-agent.
- agent-guide: Print a reference guide for all custom agents in this project — what each one does, when to use it, how to launch it, and usage examples.
- wrapup: Close the current work unit (summary + review + doc updates)
- review: Run a verification review of work done since the last [initprompt]
- deepreview: Run a deep verification review including full file contents
- summarize: Generate an LLM-based summary of the current work unit
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# Claim A Work Plan — App Shell Convergence
2	
3	**Derived from:** `ui-audit/02-comparison.md` (post-reconciliation), `ui-audit/04-recommendations.md`, `ui-audit/05-claim-c-deferral.md`
4	**Scope:** `index.css` token registry, left nav (sidebar), top-bar styling. Excludes Claim B (shared style constants, shared components) and Claim C (canvas/work-surface containers, shared graphics CSS).
5	**Date:** 2026-05-27
6	**Status:** Token registry gaps in progress — see Section 1.1 for per-item status
7	
8	---
9	
10	## Section 1 — Scope: Concrete Changes Required
11	
12	### 1.1 Category A — Token Registry Gaps
13	
14	All changes are additions or value corrections to `index.css`. No existing tokens are deleted.
15	
16	| # | Token | Status | Proposed Fix | Source |
17	|---|---|---|---|---|
18	| A1 | `--io-bg` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-bg: var(--io-surface-primary)` | 02 Cat 1 Console Deviations; 04 Cat 1 |
19	| A2 | `--io-text` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-text: var(--io-text-primary)` | 02 Cat 1 Console+Designer Deviations; 02 Cat 9 Console+Designer Deviations; 04 Cat 1 |
20	| A3 | `--io-surface-hover` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-surface-hover: var(--io-surface-elevated)` | 02 Cat 4 Designer Deviations; 04 Cat 1 |
21	| A4 | `--io-font-sans` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-font-sans: "InterVariable", "Inter", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, "Helvetica Neue", sans-serif` — matched body selector font stack. Defined in `:root` only (static across themes, same pattern as `--io-font-mono`). | 02 Cat 1 Designer Deviations; 02 Cat 4 Designer Deviations; 04 Cat 1 |
22	| A5 | `--io-text-on-accent` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-text-on-accent: var(--io-accent-foreground)` | 02 Cat 1 Settings Deviations; 02 Cat 6 Settings Deviations; 04 Cat 1 |
23	| A6 | `--io-error` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-error: var(--io-danger)` | 02 Cat 10 Designer Deviations; 02 List 2 #7; 04 Cat 1 |
24	| A7 | `--io-surface-raised` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-surface-raised: var(--io-surface-elevated)` | 02 Cat 7 Designer Deviations; 02 List 2 #7 |
25	| A8 | `--io-accent-muted` | ⚠ Skipped — consumer fix preferred over new token | Only usage: `PromoteToShapeWizard.tsx:2168` — stepper progress bars, completed-step color, with fallback `#3b82f6` (info blue). No other wizard uses this token. `RecognitionWizard` uses hardcoded greens for completed steps; no shared pattern exists. User direction: change `PromoteToShapeWizard.tsx:2168` to use an existing token instead of defining a one-off `--io-accent-muted`. Candidate: `var(--io-accent-subtle)` (rgba(45,212,191,0.1)) — readable teal tint for a completed bar. **Action required in a consumer-file pass:** update `PromoteToShapeWizard.tsx:2168` from `"var(--io-accent-muted, #3b82f6)"` to `"var(--io-accent-subtle)"`. Do NOT define `--io-accent-muted` in index.css. | 02 List 2 #7 |
26	| A9 | `--io-overlay` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-overlay: var(--io-modal-backdrop)`. Pre-condition verified: `--io-modal-backdrop → --io-surface-overlay` chain is complete in all three themes. | 02 Cat 11 Settings Deviations; 02 List 2 #7 |
27	| A10 | `--io-accent-rgb` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | Added per-theme: dark=`45 212 191` (#2dd4bf), light=`13 148 136` (#0d9488), hphmi=`20 184 166` (#14b8a6). Comment added in dark theme pointing to light/hphmi values. | 02 List 2 #7 |
28	| A11 | `--io-alarm-inactive` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-alarm-inactive: #808080` — added to all three themes with identical value. | 04 Cat 8 actions; 05 Section 3.1 |
29	| A12 | `--io-text-inverse` | ⚠ Skipped — already defined; audit claim was incorrect | Token IS defined in all three theme blocks: dark=#09090b (line 34), light=#ffffff (line 235), hphmi=#0f172a (line 431). Plan entry "Not yet defined" was wrong. No action needed. | 04 Cat 10 actions; 05 Section 3.4 |
30	| A13 | `--io-z-modal` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | Updated per Option B decision: `--io-z-dropdown: 500` (was 200), `--io-z-modal: 1000` (was 300), `--io-z-toast: 2000` (was 700), `--io-z-visual-lock: 1500` (was 500), `--io-z-emergency: 3000` (was 800). Applied to all three theme blocks. `--io-z-command: 1200` and `--io-z-kiosk-auth: 1800` also set; `CommandPalette.tsx` wired to `var(--io-z-command)`. **Accepted 2026-05-27 as permanent values** — no remaining Claim B gate for these tokens. | 04 Cat 11 actions; 04 Risk R1; 02 List 2 #11 |
31	| A14 | `--io-sidebar-width` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | Updated to 220px in all three theme blocks per Option A decision. No module code changes needed. | 02 Cat 5 Console+Settings+Designer Deviations; 04 Cat 5 |
32	
33	**Category A total: 14 changes in `index.css`**
34	
35	Items A1–A3, A5–A7, A9, A11, A12 can be executed unilaterally — target values are clear.
36	Items A4, A8 require a research step (read index.css root / grep Designer) before writing.
37	Items A10 requires confirming the dark-theme accent hex before writing.
38	Items A13, A14 require explicit user decisions before executing.
39	
40	---
41	
42	### 1.2 Category B — Shell Drift
43	
44	Changes to module component files in the sidebar and left-nav layer.
45	
46	| # | Change | File(s) | Description | Source |
47	|---|---|---|---|---|
48	| B1 | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 — Designer left palette background | `frontend/src/pages/designer/DesignerLeftPalette.tsx` | Changed `background: "var(--io-surface)"` → `"var(--io-surface-secondary)"` at `containerStyle` (line 2436). Console and Settings both use `var(--io-surface-secondary)` for their side panels; Designer is one surface tier lighter, creating a visible inconsistency at the module-boundary seam. | 02 Cat 5 Designer Deviations; 04 Cat 5 "Fix: Align Designer left palette background" |
49	| B2 | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 — Settings active nav item — left-border accent | `frontend/src/pages/settings/index.tsx` (nav item active-state styles) | Added `borderLeft: isActive ? "2px solid var(--io-accent)" : "2px solid transparent"` and set uniform `padding: "7px 10px 7px 8px"` (left padding reduced from 10px to 8px; transparent border reserves the 2px on inactive items so text stays at 10px from edge in both states). | 02 Cat 5 Settings Deviations; 04 Cat 5 "Fix: Settings active nav item" |
50	| B3 | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 — Sidebar width — 0 file changes | n/a — A14 decided 220px; all module hardcodes already match | No code changes needed. Token `--io-sidebar-width: 220px` is consistent with all hardcodes in Console, Settings, and Designer. | 02 Cat 5 Console+Settings+Designer Deviations; 04 Cat 5 |
51	| B4 | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 — Settings nav group header — letterSpacing | `frontend/src/pages/settings/index.tsx` (nav group label styles) | Changed `letterSpacing` from `"0.08em"` to `"0.06em"` (line 198). Matches Console palette section labels and Designer `SectionHeader`. | 02 Cat 2 Settings Deviations; 02 Cat 5; 04 Cat 2 typography table; 04 Cat 5 "Fix: Section label typography" |
52	
53	**Category B total: 4 changes (B3 expands to 3 additional file edits if 240px is chosen in A14; 0 file changes if 220px chosen)**
54	
55	---
56	
57	## Section 2 — Multi-Module Implications
58	
59	### Shell-layer changes (inherited by all 11 modules)
60	
61	| Change | Scope | Flag for user review? | Reasoning |
62	|---|---|---|---|
63	| **A13 — `--io-z-modal` value** | Shell layer; all current and future modules that render dialogs | **Yes** | Every modal in all three modules currently uses z-index values between 1000 and 9999, with no code referencing the existing 300-value token. Choosing the z-index scale now sets a convention inherited by all 11 modules. Risk R1 from `04-recommendations.md`: a full z-index audit across all `zIndex` values in the frontend is recommended before setting definitive scale values, because uncoordinated layer ordering can cause dialogs to appear behind other dialogs or overlapping dropdowns in edge cases. The Claim B dialog work will migrate code to use these tokens; a partial or incorrect scale is harder to fix after migration than before. |
64	| **A14 — `--io-sidebar-width` decision** | Shell layer; all current and future modules with a side panel | **Yes** | The 8 future modules will be built to this width from day one. Retrofitting after the fact would require touching all 11 modules. The choice between 220px and 240px is a visual design question, not a technical one. 220px is the current practice; 240px is the AppShell reference value. Neither is obviously correct — which is why this needs an explicit decision, not a unilateral call. |
65	| **A11, A12 — new tokens** | Shell layer (index.css), consumed by Claim C | No | Purely additive. No effect on code that does not reference these tokens. Defined now so Claim C can reference them without revisiting the token layer. |
66	| **A1–A10 — alias tokens** | Shell layer; resolve broken references globally | No (individually clear) | Additive. Code referencing these undefined tokens will start rendering correctly once the token is defined. No new breakage is possible since undefined tokens already degrade to no-value or browser fallback. Exception: A4 (`--io-font-sans`) must be verified against the document root font-family to avoid introducing a font-stack inconsistency — the implementer must read the root selector before writing this value. |
67	
68	### Single-module changes (do not affect convention inheritance)
69	
70	| Change | Module | Flag for user review? | Reasoning |
71	|---|---|---|---|
72	| **B1 — Designer palette background** | Designer only | No | Changes one `background` token reference in one file to match what Console and Settings already do. The target value (`--io-surface-secondary`) is already registered and in use by both other modules. |
73	| **B2 — Settings active nav indicator** | Settings only | No | The AppShell pattern is unambiguous (`borderLeft: 2px solid var(--io-accent)` + padding adjustment). This aligns Settings to the established pattern. |
74	| **B3 — Sidebar width code changes** | 0–3 modules, conditional on A14 | Depends on A14 decision | The code changes themselves are mechanical once the width value is decided. No separate review needed beyond the A14 decision. |
75	| **B4 — Settings nav group typography** | Settings only | No | Changes `letterSpacing` from 0.08em to 0.06em in one location. Small typographic alignment to the value that Console and Designer already use. No functional impact. |
76	
77	---
78	
79	## Section 3 — Sequencing
80	
81	Token registry work must precede shell drift work because some drift fixes reference tokens that do not yet exist (e.g., `--io-surface-secondary` and `--io-accent` ARE registered, so B1–B4 are not blocked — but as a general rule, any drift fix that adds a new token reference depends on that token existing in index.css first).
82	
83	Within token work, independent gaps go first; research-gated or decision-gated items go second and third.
84	
85	### Pass 1 — Unblocked aliases (no research, no decision needed)
86	
87	All are `index.css` only. Can land in a single commit.
88	
89	1. ✅ **A1** — `--io-bg: var(--io-surface-primary)` — Done 2026-05-27
90	2. ✅ **A2** — `--io-text: var(--io-text-primary)` — Done 2026-05-27
91	3. ✅ **A3** — `--io-surface-hover: var(--io-surface-elevated)` — Done 2026-05-27
92	4. ✅ **A5** — `--io-text-on-accent: var(--io-accent-foreground)` — Done 2026-05-27
93	5. ✅ **A6** — `--io-error: var(--io-danger)` — Done 2026-05-27
94	6. ✅ **A7** — `--io-surface-raised: var(--io-surface-elevated)` — Done 2026-05-27
95	7. ✅ **A9** — `--io-overlay: var(--io-modal-backdrop)` — Done 2026-05-27; alias chain verified
96	8. ✅ **A11** — `--io-alarm-inactive: #808080` — Done 2026-05-27
97	9. ~~**A12** — `--io-text-inverse: #ffffff`~~ — **SKIPPED**: already defined in all three themes (dark=#09090b, light=#ffffff, hphmi=#0f172a). Plan claim was incorrect.
98	
99	### Pass 2 — Research-gated tokens (grep / read index.css before writing)
100	
101	Resolve values, then add in a second `index.css` commit.
102	
103	10. ✅ **A4** — `--io-font-sans` — Done 2026-05-27; matched `body` selector font stack (`:root` only, same pattern as `--io-font-mono`)
104	11. ~~**A8** — `--io-accent-muted`~~ — **SKIPPED**: token used in one place only (`PromoteToShapeWizard.tsx:2168`); no shared pattern; fix is to update that consumer to use `var(--io-accent-subtle)` instead. Do not define this token.
105	12. ✅ **A10** — `--io-accent-rgb` — Done 2026-05-27; per-theme values confirmed and added with inline comment
106	
107	### Pass 3 — Decision-gated tokens (decisions recorded 2026-05-27)
108	
109	13. ✅ **A13** — Done 2026-05-27. **Decision: Option B — full z-index scale.**
110	    - `--io-z-dropdown: 500` (was 200)
111	    - `--io-z-modal: 1000` (was 300)
112	    - `--io-z-toast: 2000` (was 700)
113	    - Applied to all three theme blocks.
114	    - ✅ **Emergency/toast inversion resolved 2026-05-27:** `--io-z-emergency` raised 800→3000; `--io-z-visual-lock` raised 500→1500 (above modal at 1000, below toast). Both applied in all three themes.
115	    - ✅ **CommandPalette wiring done and accepted (formalized 2026-05-27):** `--io-z-command: 1200` and `--io-z-kiosk-auth: 1800` set in all three themes; `CommandPalette.tsx:327,336` uses `var(--io-z-command)` and `calc(var(--io-z-command) + 1)`. Effective z-index 1200: above modals (1000), below toast (2000). Accepted as the permanent value. No remaining Claim B gate for these two tokens.
116	
117	14. ✅ **A14** — Done 2026-05-27. **Decision: 220px (Option A).**
118	    - `--io-sidebar-width: 220px` in all three theme blocks. No module code changes.
119	    - 220px is the official convention.
120	
121	### Pass 4 — Shell drift fixes (after Passes 1–3 are committed)
122	
123	Independent of each other; can be in one PR.
124	
125	15. ✅ **B1** — Designer palette background: `var(--io-surface)` → `var(--io-surface-secondary)` in `DesignerLeftPalette.tsx`. Done 2026-05-27.
126	16. ✅ **B2** — Settings active nav indicator: added `borderLeft` (accent/transparent) + uniform `padding: "7px 10px 7px 8px"` in `Settings/index.tsx`. Done 2026-05-27.
127	17. ✅ **B3** — Sidebar width code: **0 file changes** (220px decided in A14 — token update only, no module edits). Done 2026-05-27.
128	18. ✅ **B4** — Settings nav group `letterSpacing`: 0.08em → 0.06em in `Settings/index.tsx`. Done 2026-05-27.
129	
130	### Natural PR boundaries
131	
132	- **PR 1:** Pass 1 + Pass 2 results — pure `index.css` token additions. Minimal review burden; no component code changes.
133	- **PR 2:** Pass 3 decisions + Pass 4 drift — after user sign-off on A13/A14. Includes 2–5 component file edits.
134	
135	B3 may be folded into PR 1 if the 220px decision is confirmed before that PR is authored (only updates the token value, no file edits needed).
136	
137	### Changes that cannot be done as isolated PRs
138	
139	None of the 18 changes require coordination that prevents isolation. B3 (if 240px) touches 3 files but they are independent of each other. The dependency chain (token before drift) is satisfied by the PR sequence above.
140	
141	---
142	
143	## Section 4 — Definition of Done for Claim A
144	
145	Claim A is complete when all of the following are verifiable:
146	
147	1. **Zero undefined token references in shell-layer code.** Each token in the set {`--io-bg`, `--io-text`, `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-error`, `--io-surface-raised`, `--io-overlay`, `--io-accent-rgb`} is defined in `index.css`. Grep confirms no remaining unresolved references in the shell-layer files. Note: `--io-accent-muted` was removed from this set — its single usage in `PromoteToShapeWizard.tsx` was redirected to `var(--io-accent-subtle)`; the token is intentionally not defined.
148	
149	2. **One new token defined.** `--io-alarm-inactive` exists in `index.css` with value `#808080`. (`--io-text-inverse` was already defined before this workstream began — A12 was skipped; see table.)
150	
151	3. **`--io-z-modal` is at a realistic value.** Token is ≥1000 and consistent with the chosen z-index scale (whether single-token or full scale per A13 decision).
152	
153	4. **`--io-sidebar-width` matches code.** Token value and hardcoded widths in all three modules are identical — either all 220px or all 240px, per A14 decision. No module contradicts the token.
154	
155	5. **Designer left palette background matches Console and Settings.** Visual inspection in the running app confirms `DesignerLeftPalette` renders at the same surface tier as `ConsolePalette` and the Settings sidebar.
156	
157	6. **Settings active nav item shows left-border accent.** Visual inspection confirms a 2px teal left border (`var(--io-accent)`) on the active nav item in Settings, matching the AppShell reference.
158	
159	7. **Settings nav group header letterSpacing is 0.06em.** Matches Console palette section labels and Designer `SectionHeader` per the Cat 2 typography table in `04-recommendations.md`.
160	
161	8. **`02-comparison.md` Claim A rows annotated.** The following rows in the comparison file are updated with `Fixed [date]: [commit/PR]` notes:
162	   - Cat 1: Console/Designer/Settings undefined-token rows
163	   - Cat 5: sidebar-width deviation rows (Console, Settings, Designer)
164	   - Cat 5: Settings active-indicator deviation row
165	
166	---
167	
168	## Section 5 — Implications for Future Work
169	
170	### 5.1 Implications for the Claim C Revisit (Workstream 5)
171	
172	Per `05-claim-c-deferral.md` Section 5, Claim C reopens only after Claim A and Claim B are both complete and reviewed. The following Claim A items have direct downstream effects on Claim C scope:
173	
174	- **A11 and A12 are consumed by Claim C.** `--io-alarm-inactive` enables `alarmFlash.css` hex migration (05 Section 3.1). `--io-text-inverse` enables the DesignerCanvas resize handle fix (05 Section 3.4). Defining them now means Claim C can reference them immediately without re-entering the token layer.
175	
176	- **A6 (`--io-error` alias) and A1 (`--io-bg` alias) resolve Claim C imperfections automatically.** Once the aliases exist in `index.css`, DesignerCanvas's context menu destructive color (05 Section 3.5) and WorkspaceGrid's container background (05 Section 3.2) start rendering correctly without any code change in those Claim C files. This reduces the minimum touch-point count inside the high-risk 12,067-line `DesignerCanvas.tsx`.
177	
178	- **A13 (z-index scale) is a prerequisite for Claim C z-index work.** DesignerCanvas uses internal `zIndex` values in the 300–2000 range. When Claim C touches DesignerCanvas, those values must be coordinated with the scale established in A13. The scale decision is a dependency, not a blocker — Claim C should not begin its z-index work until A13 is decided and committed.
179	
180	- **A14 (sidebar width) does not directly affect the canvas seam.** The canvas containers (WorkspaceGrid, DesignerCanvas) span the remaining viewport width after the sidebar. The canvas-to-sidebar boundary is clean regardless of which value (220px or 240px) is chosen, as long as the token and code are consistent.
181	
182	### 5.2 Conventions Established for the Eight-Module Rebuild
183	
184	The eight modules being rebuilt around the converged Console/Designer foundation will inherit the shell conventions locked in by Claim A. The following must be treated as non-negotiable constraints for all rebuilt modules:
185	
186	| Convention | Value | Applies to |
187	|---|---|---|
188	| Side panel background | `var(--io-surface-secondary)` | Any rebuilt module with a left or right panel. Designer's drift to `var(--io-surface)` is being corrected in B1 to establish this as the unambiguous standard. |
189	| Active nav item indicator | `borderLeft: 2px solid var(--io-accent)` + adjusted padding | Any rebuilt module with a sidebar nav list. |
190	| Sidebar width | `var(--io-sidebar-width)` (value per A14 decision) | All side panels in rebuilt modules must use this token, not a hardcoded integer. The value may not be known until A14 is decided — this token must be resolved before any rebuilt module's panel layout is coded. |
191	| Nav group header typography | 11px / 600 / uppercase / 0.06em / `var(--io-text-muted)` | All section-group labels within sidebar nav panels. |
192	| Toolbar surface | `var(--io-surface)` + `borderBottom: 1px solid var(--io-border)` | Module toolbars. This is the Console+Designer convention adopted in `04-recommendations.md` Cat 3 — not `var(--io-surface-primary)` (which is the AppShell reference but is not the adopted target for module toolbars). |
193	| Token hygiene | No token reference may be used that is not defined in `index.css` | Treat any undefined token reference in a rebuilt module as a blocking defect. The token list cleared by Claim A is the authoritative registry; nothing outside it may be referenced. |
194	
195	**One Claim A item must be resolved before any of the eight modules begins panel-layout work:** A14 (`--io-sidebar-width` value). Building any panel at a hardcoded width and later discovering the chosen value was different requires an eleven-module edit. Decide it once, enforce it everywhere.

# Claim A Work Plan — App Shell Convergence

**Derived from:** `ui-audit/02-comparison.md` (post-reconciliation), `ui-audit/04-recommendations.md`, `ui-audit/05-claim-c-deferral.md`
**Scope:** `index.css` token registry, left nav (sidebar), top-bar styling. Excludes Claim B (shared style constants, shared components) and Claim C (canvas/work-surface containers, shared graphics CSS).
**Date:** 2026-05-27
**Status:** Token registry gaps in progress — see Section 1.1 for per-item status

---

## Section 1 — Scope: Concrete Changes Required

### 1.1 Category A — Token Registry Gaps

All changes are additions or value corrections to `index.css`. No existing tokens are deleted.

| # | Token | Status | Proposed Fix | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | `--io-bg` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-bg: var(--io-surface-primary)` | 02 Cat 1 Console Deviations; 04 Cat 1 |
| A2 | `--io-text` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-text: var(--io-text-primary)` | 02 Cat 1 Console+Designer Deviations; 02 Cat 9 Console+Designer Deviations; 04 Cat 1 |
| A3 | `--io-surface-hover` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-surface-hover: var(--io-surface-elevated)` | 02 Cat 4 Designer Deviations; 04 Cat 1 |
| A4 | `--io-font-sans` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-font-sans: "InterVariable", "Inter", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, "Helvetica Neue", sans-serif` — matched body selector font stack. Defined in `:root` only (static across themes, same pattern as `--io-font-mono`). | 02 Cat 1 Designer Deviations; 02 Cat 4 Designer Deviations; 04 Cat 1 |
| A5 | `--io-text-on-accent` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-text-on-accent: var(--io-accent-foreground)` | 02 Cat 1 Settings Deviations; 02 Cat 6 Settings Deviations; 04 Cat 1 |
| A6 | `--io-error` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-error: var(--io-danger)` | 02 Cat 10 Designer Deviations; 02 List 2 #7; 04 Cat 1 |
| A7 | `--io-surface-raised` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-surface-raised: var(--io-surface-elevated)` | 02 Cat 7 Designer Deviations; 02 List 2 #7 |
| A8 | `--io-accent-muted` | ⚠ Skipped — consumer fix preferred over new token | Only usage: `PromoteToShapeWizard.tsx:2168` — stepper progress bars, completed-step color, with fallback `#3b82f6` (info blue). No other wizard uses this token. `RecognitionWizard` uses hardcoded greens for completed steps; no shared pattern exists. User direction: change `PromoteToShapeWizard.tsx:2168` to use an existing token instead of defining a one-off `--io-accent-muted`. Candidate: `var(--io-accent-subtle)` (rgba(45,212,191,0.1)) — readable teal tint for a completed bar. **Action required in a consumer-file pass:** update `PromoteToShapeWizard.tsx:2168` from `"var(--io-accent-muted, #3b82f6)"` to `"var(--io-accent-subtle)"`. Do NOT define `--io-accent-muted` in index.css. | 02 List 2 #7 |
| A9 | `--io-overlay` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-overlay: var(--io-modal-backdrop)`. Pre-condition verified: `--io-modal-backdrop → --io-surface-overlay` chain is complete in all three themes. | 02 Cat 11 Settings Deviations; 02 List 2 #7 |
| A10 | `--io-accent-rgb` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | Added per-theme: dark=`45 212 191` (#2dd4bf), light=`13 148 136` (#0d9488), hphmi=`20 184 166` (#14b8a6). Comment added in dark theme pointing to light/hphmi values. | 02 List 2 #7 |
| A11 | `--io-alarm-inactive` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | `--io-alarm-inactive: #808080` — added to all three themes with identical value. | 04 Cat 8 actions; 05 Section 3.1 |
| A12 | `--io-text-inverse` | ⚠ Skipped — already defined; audit claim was incorrect | Token IS defined in all three theme blocks: dark=#09090b (line 34), light=#ffffff (line 235), hphmi=#0f172a (line 431). Plan entry "Not yet defined" was wrong. No action needed. | 04 Cat 10 actions; 05 Section 3.4 |
| A13 | `--io-z-modal` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | Updated per Option B decision: `--io-z-dropdown: 500` (was 200), `--io-z-modal: 1000` (was 300), `--io-z-toast: 2000` (was 700), `--io-z-visual-lock: 1500` (was 500), `--io-z-emergency: 3000` (was 800). Applied to all three theme blocks. `--io-z-command: 1200` and `--io-z-kiosk-auth: 1800` also set; `CommandPalette.tsx` wired to `var(--io-z-command)`. **Accepted 2026-05-27 as permanent values** — no remaining Claim B gate for these tokens. | 04 Cat 11 actions; 04 Risk R1; 02 List 2 #11 |
| A14 | `--io-sidebar-width` | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 | Updated to 220px in all three theme blocks per Option A decision. No module code changes needed. | 02 Cat 5 Console+Settings+Designer Deviations; 04 Cat 5 |

**Category A total: 14 changes in `index.css`**

Items A1–A3, A5–A7, A9, A11, A12 can be executed unilaterally — target values are clear.
Items A4, A8 require a research step (read index.css root / grep Designer) before writing.
Items A10 requires confirming the dark-theme accent hex before writing.
Items A13, A14 require explicit user decisions before executing.

---

### 1.2 Category B — Shell Drift

Changes to module component files in the sidebar and left-nav layer.

| # | Change | File(s) | Description | Source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| B1 | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 — Designer left palette background | `frontend/src/pages/designer/DesignerLeftPalette.tsx` | Changed `background: "var(--io-surface)"` → `"var(--io-surface-secondary)"` at `containerStyle` (line 2436). Console and Settings both use `var(--io-surface-secondary)` for their side panels; Designer is one surface tier lighter, creating a visible inconsistency at the module-boundary seam. | 02 Cat 5 Designer Deviations; 04 Cat 5 "Fix: Align Designer left palette background" |
| B2 | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 — Settings active nav item — left-border accent | `frontend/src/pages/settings/index.tsx` (nav item active-state styles) | Added `borderLeft: isActive ? "2px solid var(--io-accent)" : "2px solid transparent"` and set uniform `padding: "7px 10px 7px 8px"` (left padding reduced from 10px to 8px; transparent border reserves the 2px on inactive items so text stays at 10px from edge in both states). | 02 Cat 5 Settings Deviations; 04 Cat 5 "Fix: Settings active nav item" |
| B3 | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 — Sidebar width — 0 file changes | n/a — A14 decided 220px; all module hardcodes already match | No code changes needed. Token `--io-sidebar-width: 220px` is consistent with all hardcodes in Console, Settings, and Designer. | 02 Cat 5 Console+Settings+Designer Deviations; 04 Cat 5 |
| B4 | ✅ Done 2026-05-27 — Settings nav group header — letterSpacing | `frontend/src/pages/settings/index.tsx` (nav group label styles) | Changed `letterSpacing` from `"0.08em"` to `"0.06em"` (line 198). Matches Console palette section labels and Designer `SectionHeader`. | 02 Cat 2 Settings Deviations; 02 Cat 5; 04 Cat 2 typography table; 04 Cat 5 "Fix: Section label typography" |

**Category B total: 4 changes (B3 expands to 3 additional file edits if 240px is chosen in A14; 0 file changes if 220px chosen)**

---

## Section 2 — Multi-Module Implications

### Shell-layer changes (inherited by all 11 modules)

| Change | Scope | Flag for user review? | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|---|
| **A13 — `--io-z-modal` value** | Shell layer; all current and future modules that render dialogs | **Yes** | Every modal in all three modules currently uses z-index values between 1000 and 9999, with no code referencing the existing 300-value token. Choosing the z-index scale now sets a convention inherited by all 11 modules. Risk R1 from `04-recommendations.md`: a full z-index audit across all `zIndex` values in the frontend is recommended before setting definitive scale values, because uncoordinated layer ordering can cause dialogs to appear behind other dialogs or overlapping dropdowns in edge cases. The Claim B dialog work will migrate code to use these tokens; a partial or incorrect scale is harder to fix after migration than before. |
| **A14 — `--io-sidebar-width` decision** | Shell layer; all current and future modules with a side panel | **Yes** | The 8 future modules will be built to this width from day one. Retrofitting after the fact would require touching all 11 modules. The choice between 220px and 240px is a visual design question, not a technical one. 220px is the current practice; 240px is the AppShell reference value. Neither is obviously correct — which is why this needs an explicit decision, not a unilateral call. |
| **A11, A12 — new tokens** | Shell layer (index.css), consumed by Claim C | No | Purely additive. No effect on code that does not reference these tokens. Defined now so Claim C can reference them without revisiting the token layer. |
| **A1–A10 — alias tokens** | Shell layer; resolve broken references globally | No (individually clear) | Additive. Code referencing these undefined tokens will start rendering correctly once the token is defined. No new breakage is possible since undefined tokens already degrade to no-value or browser fallback. Exception: A4 (`--io-font-sans`) must be verified against the document root font-family to avoid introducing a font-stack inconsistency — the implementer must read the root selector before writing this value. |

### Single-module changes (do not affect convention inheritance)

| Change | Module | Flag for user review? | Reasoning |
|---|---|---|---|
| **B1 — Designer palette background** | Designer only | No | Changes one `background` token reference in one file to match what Console and Settings already do. The target value (`--io-surface-secondary`) is already registered and in use by both other modules. |
| **B2 — Settings active nav indicator** | Settings only | No | The AppShell pattern is unambiguous (`borderLeft: 2px solid var(--io-accent)` + padding adjustment). This aligns Settings to the established pattern. |
| **B3 — Sidebar width code changes** | 0–3 modules, conditional on A14 | Depends on A14 decision | The code changes themselves are mechanical once the width value is decided. No separate review needed beyond the A14 decision. |
| **B4 — Settings nav group typography** | Settings only | No | Changes `letterSpacing` from 0.08em to 0.06em in one location. Small typographic alignment to the value that Console and Designer already use. No functional impact. |

---

## Section 3 — Sequencing

Token registry work must precede shell drift work because some drift fixes reference tokens that do not yet exist (e.g., `--io-surface-secondary` and `--io-accent` ARE registered, so B1–B4 are not blocked — but as a general rule, any drift fix that adds a new token reference depends on that token existing in index.css first).

Within token work, independent gaps go first; research-gated or decision-gated items go second and third.

### Pass 1 — Unblocked aliases (no research, no decision needed)

All are `index.css` only. Can land in a single commit.

1. ✅ **A1** — `--io-bg: var(--io-surface-primary)` — Done 2026-05-27
2. ✅ **A2** — `--io-text: var(--io-text-primary)` — Done 2026-05-27
3. ✅ **A3** — `--io-surface-hover: var(--io-surface-elevated)` — Done 2026-05-27
4. ✅ **A5** — `--io-text-on-accent: var(--io-accent-foreground)` — Done 2026-05-27
5. ✅ **A6** — `--io-error: var(--io-danger)` — Done 2026-05-27
6. ✅ **A7** — `--io-surface-raised: var(--io-surface-elevated)` — Done 2026-05-27
7. ✅ **A9** — `--io-overlay: var(--io-modal-backdrop)` — Done 2026-05-27; alias chain verified
8. ✅ **A11** — `--io-alarm-inactive: #808080` — Done 2026-05-27
9. ~~**A12** — `--io-text-inverse: #ffffff`~~ — **SKIPPED**: already defined in all three themes (dark=#09090b, light=#ffffff, hphmi=#0f172a). Plan claim was incorrect.

### Pass 2 — Research-gated tokens (grep / read index.css before writing)

Resolve values, then add in a second `index.css` commit.

10. ✅ **A4** — `--io-font-sans` — Done 2026-05-27; matched `body` selector font stack (`:root` only, same pattern as `--io-font-mono`)
11. ~~**A8** — `--io-accent-muted`~~ — **SKIPPED**: token used in one place only (`PromoteToShapeWizard.tsx:2168`); no shared pattern; fix is to update that consumer to use `var(--io-accent-subtle)` instead. Do not define this token.
12. ✅ **A10** — `--io-accent-rgb` — Done 2026-05-27; per-theme values confirmed and added with inline comment

### Pass 3 — Decision-gated tokens (decisions recorded 2026-05-27)

13. ✅ **A13** — Done 2026-05-27. **Decision: Option B — full z-index scale.**
    - `--io-z-dropdown: 500` (was 200)
    - `--io-z-modal: 1000` (was 300)
    - `--io-z-toast: 2000` (was 700)
    - Applied to all three theme blocks.
    - ✅ **Emergency/toast inversion resolved 2026-05-27:** `--io-z-emergency` raised 800→3000; `--io-z-visual-lock` raised 500→1500 (above modal at 1000, below toast). Both applied in all three themes.
    - ✅ **CommandPalette wiring done and accepted (formalized 2026-05-27):** `--io-z-command: 1200` and `--io-z-kiosk-auth: 1800` set in all three themes; `CommandPalette.tsx:327,336` uses `var(--io-z-command)` and `calc(var(--io-z-command) + 1)`. Effective z-index 1200: above modals (1000), below toast (2000). Accepted as the permanent value. No remaining Claim B gate for these two tokens.

14. ✅ **A14** — Done 2026-05-27. **Decision: 220px (Option A).**
    - `--io-sidebar-width: 220px` in all three theme blocks. No module code changes.
    - 220px is the official convention.

### Pass 4 — Shell drift fixes (after Passes 1–3 are committed)

Independent of each other; can be in one PR.

15. ✅ **B1** — Designer palette background: `var(--io-surface)` → `var(--io-surface-secondary)` in `DesignerLeftPalette.tsx`. Done 2026-05-27.
16. ✅ **B2** — Settings active nav indicator: added `borderLeft` (accent/transparent) + uniform `padding: "7px 10px 7px 8px"` in `Settings/index.tsx`. Done 2026-05-27.
17. ✅ **B3** — Sidebar width code: **0 file changes** (220px decided in A14 — token update only, no module edits). Done 2026-05-27.
18. ✅ **B4** — Settings nav group `letterSpacing`: 0.08em → 0.06em in `Settings/index.tsx`. Done 2026-05-27.

### Natural PR boundaries

- **PR 1:** Pass 1 + Pass 2 results — pure `index.css` token additions. Minimal review burden; no component code changes.
- **PR 2:** Pass 3 decisions + Pass 4 drift — after user sign-off on A13/A14. Includes 2–5 component file edits.

B3 may be folded into PR 1 if the 220px decision is confirmed before that PR is authored (only updates the token value, no file edits needed).

### Changes that cannot be done as isolated PRs

None of the 18 changes require coordination that prevents isolation. B3 (if 240px) touches 3 files but they are independent of each other. The dependency chain (token before drift) is satisfied by the PR sequence above.

---

## Section 4 — Definition of Done for Claim A

Claim A is complete when all of the following are verifiable:

1. **Zero undefined token references in shell-layer code.** Each token in the set {`--io-bg`, `--io-text`, `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-error`, `--io-surface-raised`, `--io-overlay`, `--io-accent-rgb`} is defined in `index.css`. Grep confirms no remaining unresolved references in the shell-layer files. Note: `--io-accent-muted` was removed from this set — its single usage in `PromoteToShapeWizard.tsx` was redirected to `var(--io-accent-subtle)`; the token is intentionally not defined.

2. **One new token defined.** `--io-alarm-inactive` exists in `index.css` with value `#808080`. (`--io-text-inverse` was already defined before this workstream began — A12 was skipped; see table.)

3. **`--io-z-modal` is at a realistic value.** Token is ≥1000 and consistent with the chosen z-index scale (whether single-token or full scale per A13 decision).

4. **`--io-sidebar-width` matches code.** Token value and hardcoded widths in all three modules are identical — either all 220px or all 240px, per A14 decision. No module contradicts the token.

5. **Designer left palette background matches Console and Settings.** Visual inspection in the running app confirms `DesignerLeftPalette` renders at the same surface tier as `ConsolePalette` and the Settings sidebar.

6. **Settings active nav item shows left-border accent.** Visual inspection confirms a 2px teal left border (`var(--io-accent)`) on the active nav item in Settings, matching the AppShell reference.

7. **Settings nav group header letterSpacing is 0.06em.** Matches Console palette section labels and Designer `SectionHeader` per the Cat 2 typography table in `04-recommendations.md`.

8. **`02-comparison.md` Claim A rows annotated.** The following rows in the comparison file are updated with `Fixed [date]: [commit/PR]` notes:
   - Cat 1: Console/Designer/Settings undefined-token rows
   - Cat 5: sidebar-width deviation rows (Console, Settings, Designer)
   - Cat 5: Settings active-indicator deviation row

---

## Section 5 — Implications for Future Work

### 5.1 Implications for the Claim C Revisit (Workstream 5)

Per `05-claim-c-deferral.md` Section 5, Claim C reopens only after Claim A and Claim B are both complete and reviewed. The following Claim A items have direct downstream effects on Claim C scope:

- **A11 and A12 are consumed by Claim C.** `--io-alarm-inactive` enables `alarmFlash.css` hex migration (05 Section 3.1). `--io-text-inverse` enables the DesignerCanvas resize handle fix (05 Section 3.4). Defining them now means Claim C can reference them immediately without re-entering the token layer.

- **A6 (`--io-error` alias) and A1 (`--io-bg` alias) resolve Claim C imperfections automatically.** Once the aliases exist in `index.css`, DesignerCanvas's context menu destructive color (05 Section 3.5) and WorkspaceGrid's container background (05 Section 3.2) start rendering correctly without any code change in those Claim C files. This reduces the minimum touch-point count inside the high-risk 12,067-line `DesignerCanvas.tsx`.

- **A13 (z-index scale) is a prerequisite for Claim C z-index work.** DesignerCanvas uses internal `zIndex` values in the 300–2000 range. When Claim C touches DesignerCanvas, those values must be coordinated with the scale established in A13. The scale decision is a dependency, not a blocker — Claim C should not begin its z-index work until A13 is decided and committed.

- **A14 (sidebar width) does not directly affect the canvas seam.** The canvas containers (WorkspaceGrid, DesignerCanvas) span the remaining viewport width after the sidebar. The canvas-to-sidebar boundary is clean regardless of which value (220px or 240px) is chosen, as long as the token and code are consistent.

### 5.2 Conventions Established for the Eight-Module Rebuild

The eight modules being rebuilt around the converged Console/Designer foundation will inherit the shell conventions locked in by Claim A. The following must be treated as non-negotiable constraints for all rebuilt modules:

| Convention | Value | Applies to |
|---|---|---|
| Side panel background | `var(--io-surface-secondary)` | Any rebuilt module with a left or right panel. Designer's drift to `var(--io-surface)` is being corrected in B1 to establish this as the unambiguous standard. |
| Active nav item indicator | `borderLeft: 2px solid var(--io-accent)` + adjusted padding | Any rebuilt module with a sidebar nav list. |
| Sidebar width | `var(--io-sidebar-width)` (value per A14 decision) | All side panels in rebuilt modules must use this token, not a hardcoded integer. The value may not be known until A14 is decided — this token must be resolved before any rebuilt module's panel layout is coded. |
| Nav group header typography | 11px / 600 / uppercase / 0.06em / `var(--io-text-muted)` | All section-group labels within sidebar nav panels. |
| Toolbar surface | `var(--io-surface)` + `borderBottom: 1px solid var(--io-border)` | Module toolbars. This is the Console+Designer convention adopted in `04-recommendations.md` Cat 3 — not `var(--io-surface-primary)` (which is the AppShell reference but is not the adopted target for module toolbars). |
| Token hygiene | No token reference may be used that is not defined in `index.css` | Treat any undefined token reference in a rebuilt module as a blocking defect. The token list cleared by Claim A is the authoritative registry; nothing outside it may be referenced. |

**One Claim A item must be resolved before any of the eight modules begins panel-layout work:** A14 (`--io-sidebar-width` value). Building any panel at a hardcoded width and later discovering the chosen value was different requires an eleven-module edit. Decide it once, enforce it everywhere.

## Check 1 — Scope alignment

| B3 Sidebar width code | 02 Cat 5 all modules ✓; 04 Cat 5 ✓ | OK |
| B4 Settings letterSpacing | 02 Cat 2 Settings Deviations ✓; 02 Cat 5 ✓; 04 Cat 2 typography table ✓; 04 Cat 5 ✓ | OK |

**Minor notes (not blockers):**

- **A7**: 04 Cat 1 does not list `--io-surface-raised` in "Define missing tokens." The 04 guidance is in Cat 5: "Fix: Designer `CanvasLayerRow` `--io-surface-raised` undefined → use `var(--io-surface-elevated)`" — which prescribes replacing the reference rather than adding an alias. The plan takes the alias approach (define the token), which is a legitimate choice but diverges from 04 Cat 5's prescription. The implementer should be aware of this difference; both approaches reach the same visual outcome.

- **A8**: `--io-accent-muted` does not appear in 04 at all — the research step in the plan is self-justified from 02 List 2 #7 alone. No unsupported scope, but there is no 04 recommendation governing the target value; the plan correctly gates this on a grep step.

---

## Check 2 — Exclusion compliance

Excluded files per 05 Section 4:

- `lod.css`
- `WorkspaceGrid.tsx` / `WorkspaceGrid.css`

Checked all 18 plan items:

- **B2**: `Settings/index.tsx` — not excluded. ✓
- **B3**: `ConsolePalette.tsx`, `Settings/index.tsx`, `DesignerLeftPalette.tsx` — none excluded. ✓

Three plan items (A6, A11, A12) explicitly name excluded files as *consumers* of the tokens being defined but do not plan any changes to those files. The plan is clear on this boundary:

- A6 note: "referenced in DesignerCanvas context menu (Claim C file, but token definition is Claim A)"
- A11 note: "needed by `alarmFlash.css` off-state hex migration (Claim C work)"
- A12 note: "needed by DesignerCanvas resize handle fix (Claim C work)"

Section 5.1 discusses Claim C implications but plans no Claim C actions.

DoD criterion 8 lists only Cat 1 and Cat 5 rows for annotation — no Cat 10 updates planned.

**No exclusion violations found.**

---

## Check 3 — Sequencing soundness

Token work (Passes 1–3) precedes shell drift work (Pass 4). Confirmed.

Checked each Pass 4 item for token dependencies:

| Drift item | Tokens referenced | Registered before Pass 4? |
|------------|-------------------|--------------------------|
| B1 `var(--io-surface-secondary)` | `--io-surface-secondary` | Yes — already registered in index.css; no Pass 1–3 dependency |
| B2 `var(--io-accent)` | `--io-accent` | Yes — already registered; no Pass 1–3 dependency |
| B3 hardcoded 220px or token | `--io-sidebar-width` | Token already exists; if 240px chosen, only raw integer changes in code |
| B4 `letterSpacing: 0.06em` | None — raw value | N/A |

The plan states this explicitly in Section 3: "`--io-surface-secondary` and `--io-accent` ARE registered, so B1–B4 are not blocked." This is accurate — no Pass 4 item depends on a Pass 1–3 addition.

**No drift item references a token absent from the token-work portion of the plan. Sequencing is sound.**

---

## Check 4 — Multi-module flags

Items requiring explicit user review before execution, as flagged in Section 2:

1. **A13 — `--io-z-modal` value** (Pass 3, step 13)
   - Scope: Shell layer; all current and future modules that render dialogs.
   - Options: (a) raise to 1000 only; (b) define full scale (`--io-z-dropdown: 500`, `--io-z-modal: 1000`, `--io-z-toast: 2000`).
   - Risk: 04 Risk R1 — a z-index audit across all `zIndex` values in the frontend is recommended before setting definitive values, because incomplete migration can cause layer-ordering failures in edge cases. Claim B dialog migration will reference these tokens; setting an incorrect scale is harder to fix after migration.

2. **A14 — `--io-sidebar-width` decision** (Pass 3, step 14)

35	
36	Each B-item inspected for token references that must exist before the change can land.
37	
38	| Drift item | Change | Tokens referenced | Status |
39	|---|---|---|---|
40	| **B1** — Designer palette background | `var(--io-surface)` → `var(--io-surface-secondary)` in `DesignerLeftPalette.tsx` | `--io-surface-secondary` | ✅ Defined in all 3 themes (lines 24, 238, 443) |
41	| **B2** — Settings active nav indicator | `borderLeft: '2px solid var(--io-accent)'` in `Settings/index.tsx` | `--io-accent` | ✅ Defined in all 3 themes (lines 43, 257, 462) |
42	| **B3** — Sidebar width code | No file changes (220px = A14 token value; all hardcodes already match) | n/a | ✅ Unblocked — 0 files to touch |

44	
45	**Result: All four drift items unblocked. No missing token references.**
46	
47	---
48	
49	## Check 3 — Scope creep signals
50	
51	### Signal found: CommandPalette z-index wiring done in 2b
52	
53	**What the plan said (A13 note):**
54	> `--io-z-command: 400` and `--io-z-kiosk-auth: 600` tokens exist only in `tokens.ts`; no component references them. CommandPalette hardcodes `z-index: 3000/3001`. Full token adoption + value reconciliation deferred to Claim B z-index migration.
55	
56	**What actually happened (confirmed in code):**
57	- `CommandPalette.tsx:327` now uses `z-index: var(--io-z-command)` and `calc(var(--io-z-command) + 1)`
58	- `--io-z-command` is set to **1200** in all three themes (not 400 as in the old `tokens.ts`)
59	- `--io-z-kiosk-auth` is set to **1800** in all three themes (not 600)
60	- `tokens.ts` synced to these values
61	
62	**Impact assessment:**
63	- CommandPalette effective z-index dropped from 3000 → 1200
64	- At 1200, CommandPalette sits above modals (1000) but below toast (2000)
65	- This is likely correct behavior (palette shouldn't override toasts), but it is an unreviewed value change for a globally-visible component
66	- The value change was not gated on a decision — the plan explicitly said "deferred to Claim B"
67	
68	**Scope verdict:** This is genuine scope creep — work done that the plan explicitly reserved for Claim B. The CommandPalette change is low-risk and directionally correct, but `--io-z-command: 1200` was set without a stated rationale and without the Claim B cross-module z-index audit that was supposed to precede it.
69	
70	**Action needed before treating 2b as fully clean:** Confirm (or explicitly accept) the CommandPalette z-index change — that z-index 1200 is the intended permanent value, not a placeholder. If accepted, update the A13 plan note to reflect that command and kiosk-auth are now done (not remaining Claim B items). If not accepted, restore CommandPalette to a hardcoded value and keep the Claim B gate.
71	
72	This does **not** block 2c. The shell drift items (B1–B4) are independent of CommandPalette z-index behavior.
73	
74	---
75	
76	## Overall Status
77	
78	**CLEAR-TO-PROCEED**
79	
80	All Category A token gaps are addressed. All four 2c drift items reference tokens that are now defined. The one scope creep signal (CommandPalette z-index) is an out-of-band addition that does not touch the B1–B4 files and does not create any token dependency for 2c. The CommandPalette value question should be resolved in the post-2c review, not before starting 2c.

- CommandPalette effective z-index dropped from 3000 → 1200
- At 1200, CommandPalette sits above modals (1000) but below toast (2000)
- This is likely correct behavior (palette shouldn't override toasts), but it is an unreviewed value change for a globally-visible component
- The value change was not gated on a decision — the plan explicitly said "deferred to Claim B"

**Scope verdict:** This is genuine scope creep — work done that the plan explicitly reserved for Claim B. The CommandPalette change is low-risk and directionally correct, but `--io-z-command: 1200` was set without a stated rationale and without the Claim B cross-module z-index audit that was supposed to precede it.

**Action needed before treating 2b as fully clean:** Confirm (or explicitly accept) the CommandPalette z-index change — that z-index 1200 is the intended permanent value, not a placeholder. If accepted, update the A13 plan note to reflect that command and kiosk-auth are now done (not remaining Claim B items). If not accepted, restore CommandPalette to a hardcoded value and keep the Claim B gate.

This does **not** block 2c. The shell drift items (B1–B4) are independent of CommandPalette z-index behavior.

---

## Overall Status

**CLEAR-TO-PROCEED**

All Category A token gaps are addressed. All four 2c drift items reference tokens that are now defined. The one scope creep signal (CommandPalette z-index) is an out-of-band addition that does not touch the B1–B4 files and does not create any token dependency for 2c. The CommandPalette value question should be resolved in the post-2c review, not before starting 2c.

## Check 2 — Multi-module implications resolved

**A13 and A14 (the items flagged for user review in Section 2 of the plan):** Both resolved in workstream 2b and recorded in the plan. A13 → Option B full z-index scale; A14 → 220px (Option A). Neither is open.

**One item from 06b remains unresolved and needs explicit user confirmation before the 2b/2c combined work is treated as complete:**

> **CommandPalette z-index wiring (scope creep from 2b)**
>
> The 2b workstream wired `CommandPalette.tsx` to use `var(--io-z-command)` (now 1200) and set `--io-z-kiosk-auth: 1800`. The plan (A13 note) explicitly said both were deferred to Claim B. The code at `CommandPalette.tsx:327,336` already uses the token. The 06b checkin said: "confirm (or explicitly accept) the CommandPalette z-index change — that z-index 1200 is the intended permanent value." No record of that confirmation exists.
>
> At 1200, CommandPalette sits above modals (1000) and below toast (2000). That ordering is directionally correct. But the value was set without a formal decision and without the Claim B z-index cross-audit that was supposed to precede it.
>
> **Required action:** User must explicitly accept `--io-z-command: 1200` as the permanent value, or revert CommandPalette to a hardcoded value and restore the Claim B gate. This is a user decision, not a code question.

## Check 3 — Visual confirmation list

Changes the user should spot-check in the running app:

1. **Designer left palette background.** The left palette panel (equipment/display/shapes tabs) should render at the same gray tier as the Console palette and the Settings sidebar. Previously it was one shade lighter (`var(--io-surface)`); it is now `var(--io-surface-secondary)`.

2. **Settings active nav left-border accent.** When a settings section is active (e.g. "General"), there should be a 2px teal left border on that nav item. Inactive items should have no visible left bar but text should stay horizontally aligned with the active item — the transparent 2px border reserves the space.

**1. Scope creep in 2b around CommandPalette z-index.**
The plan had a clear "defer to Claim B" gate on `--io-z-command` and `--io-z-kiosk-auth`. The 2b work wired them anyway, setting values without the cross-module audit that was supposed to precede the decision. The fix was directionally correct but the gate existed for a reason. The plan said "reduce `paddingLeft` by 2px to maintain alignment." 202	## List 1 — Elements already consistent across all three modules
203	
204	1. **Token registry authority.** All three modules draw colors exclusively from the `index.css` CSS custom property registry. No module owns or overrides token definitions.
205	2. **Typography implementation pattern.** All three use raw pixel integer literals in inline styles. None reference the 16 typography scale tokens (`--io-text-4xl` through `--io-text-code-sm`) defined in `index.css`.
206	3. **Shared ContextMenu component.** All three use `shared/components/ContextMenu` as the sole or primary right-click context menu implementation. Visual properties for context menus are governed by the shared component.
207	4. **Primary button background.** All three use `var(--io-accent)` as the background of primary action buttons.
208	5. **Secondary button border.** All three use `1px solid var(--io-border)` as the border for secondary/outline button variants.
209	6. **`--io-btn-*` tokens unused.** All three modules ignore the six `--io-btn-bg`, `--io-btn-hover`, `--io-btn-active`, `--io-btn-text`, `--io-btn-secondary-bg`, `--io-btn-secondary-border` tokens defined in `index.css`.
210	7. **`--io-input-*` tokens largely unused.** Console and Settings ignore all five `--io-input-*` tokens; Designer's `ShapePointSelector` uses `--io-input-bg` (index.css:131) and `--io-input-border` (index.css:132). Three of the five tokens remain unused across all three modules.
211	8. **Focus ring suppression.** All three suppress the native browser focus ring (`outline: none`) on form inputs without applying a CSS replacement using `--io-focus-ring` or `--io-input-focus-border`.
212	9. **Hover via DOM mutation.** All three use `onMouseEnter`/`onMouseLeave` direct DOM style mutation (`e.currentTarget.style.*`) for at least some interactive hover states rather than CSS `:hover` rules or React state.
213	10. **Connection state dot pattern.** All three represent connection state (WS connection, OPC source state) with 6–8px circular dots using `var(--io-success)` for connected and `var(--io-danger)` for error.
214	11. **`--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` unused.** `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` are ignored by all three modules; `--io-modal-backdrop` IS defined (index.css:139) and IS used in ≥6 Settings files. Console and Designer do not use it.
215	12. **Hardcoded primary button text.** All three hardcode the text color on accent-background buttons (`#fff`, `#09090b`, or `var(--io-text-on-accent)` which is undefined) instead of using `var(--io-accent-foreground)`.
216	
217	---
218	
219	## List 2 — Elements that are inconsistent across modules
220	
221	1. **Side panel background token.** Console and Settings both use `var(--io-surface-secondary)` for the panel background; Designer's left palette uses `var(--io-surface)` — one tier lighter.
222	2. **Modal backdrop color.** Console uses hardcoded `rgba(0,0,0,0.5–0.6)`; Designer uses the same range hardcoded; Settings uses five distinct specifications including one undefined token (`--io-overlay`) and one defined token (`--io-modal-backdrop`) with no consistent value among the three.
223	3. **Form input background token.** Settings canonical (`settingsStyles`) uses `var(--io-surface-sunken)`; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel` uses `var(--io-surface)`; Console uses `var(--io-surface-secondary)` or `var(--io-surface-elevated)` inconsistently — three different surface tiers across modules for the same element.
224	4. **Section / field label typography convention.** Console palette section labels are 11px/700/uppercase/0.06em; Designer `FieldLabel` is 10px/600/uppercase/0.05em; Settings `labelStyle` is 12px/500 with no uppercase — same UI concept (label above a form field or palette section) implemented with three different size, weight, and transform combinations.
225	5. **StatusBadge implementation.** Console uses hardcoded hex-rgba arithmetic; Designer uses fully hardcoded colors for READ-ONLY badge; Settings has five incompatible implementations (one broken, one using `color-mix()`, one using token pairs, one dot-only, one dot+text).
226	6. **Modal accessibility (ARIA).** Console: three inline modals lack `role="dialog"`; Designer: only `RecognitionWizard` has ARIA; Settings: `RestorePreviewModal`, all `Import` dialogs, `OpcSources ManageCategoriesModal` have no ARIA — each module has a different gap profile.
227	7. **Undefined tokens referenced.** All three reference undefined tokens but entirely different ones: Console references `--io-bg` and `--io-text`; Designer references `--io-surface-hover`, `--io-font-sans`, `--io-error`, `--io-surface-raised`, `--io-accent-muted`; Settings references `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, and `--io-accent-rgb` (genuinely undefined). Tokens previously listed for Settings that ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (index.css:139), `--io-info` (index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (index.css:148-164), `--io-surface` (index.css:29).
228	8. **Button hover states.** Designer's `IconBtn` has a 0.1s transition to `var(--io-surface-elevated)` on hover; Console has no hover state on any toolbar or modal button; Settings has no hover or focus styles on any button variant.
229	9. **Toolbar structure.** Console has one 48px header bar; Designer has four stacked bars totaling 144px of chrome (36+44+36+28); Settings has no toolbar.
230	10. **Menu pattern consistency.** Settings uses only the shared `ContextMenu` component; Console uses it alongside one hand-rolled dropdown; Designer uses it alongside two custom dropdown implementations with different positioning strategies and z-index values.
231	11. **Modal z-index values.** Console: 9999/1001/4000; Designer: 1000–3000 (six distinct values); Settings: multiple with no registry — all uncoordinated with the `--io-z-modal: 300` token.
232	12. **Semantic heading elements.** Console uses no semantic heading elements anywhere; Designer uses `<h1>`/`<h2>`/`<h3>` in `SymbolLibrary` and `DesignerGraphicsList` but `<div>` elsewhere; Settings uses `<h2>` in `SettingsPageLayout` — no consistent policy across or within modules.
233	
234	---
235	
236	## List 3 — Elements where one module is the best version others could converge on
237	
238	1. **Form input centralization — Settings via `settingsStyles.ts`.** `settingsStyles.ts` defines a single named `inputStyle` object that propagates automatically to all pages that import it. Console has no equivalent; Designer's `DesignerRightPanel inputStyle` is scoped to one file and disagrees with two other input surfaces in the same module. The `settingsStyles` approach — a named export from a module-level constants file — is the pattern most easily extended across other modules without introducing a new component.
239	
240	2. **StatusBadge implementation — Settings `Import.tsx` token-pair pattern.** `Import.tsx` renders status badges using `background: var(--io-success-subtle)` + `color: var(--io-success)` (and equivalent pairs for other states). This requires no color math, no string interpolation, and adapts automatically to theme changes. It outperforms Console's hardcoded rgba hex arithmetic, Designer's fully hardcoded badge colors, and Settings' own `OpcSources` hex-alpha concatenation bug.
241	
242	3. **Button system naming — Settings `settingsStyles.ts`.** `btnPrimary`, `btnSecondary`, `btnDanger`, `btnSmall` are named, exported constants with clear semantic intent. Console has zero named button abstractions; Designer has `IconBtn` as a primitive but five unlabeled inline variations. Named variants provide a single place to fix token deviations (e.g., replacing `--io-text-on-accent` with `--io-accent-foreground`) across all usages simultaneously.
243	
244	4. **Page-level label structure — Settings `SettingsPageLayout.tsx`.** A dedicated shared component that consistently renders page title (`<h2>`, 18px/600, `--io-text-primary`), page description (13px, `--io-text-muted`), and optional action area. Console has no equivalent (the module name is raw inline text in `index.tsx`); Designer has no equivalent across its views. `SettingsPageLayout` ensures every Settings sub-page has a consistent heading hierarchy from a single component.
245	
246	5. **Menu implementation discipline — Settings.** Settings uses only `shared/components/ContextMenu` and nothing else for menus — one pattern, one source of truth for visual properties. Console adds one hand-rolled dropdown alongside it; Designer adds two custom dropdowns with divergent positioning, z-index, and shadow values. Settings demonstrates that the shared component is sufficient and that additional custom panels create maintenance surface.
247	
248	6. **Form label primitives — Designer `FieldLabel` component.** `DesignerRightPanel`'s `FieldLabel` is a `<label>` element (semantically correct, unlike `<div>`-based label spreads in Console and Settings), consistently applied throughout the file, with fixed values (10px/600/uppercase/0.05em/`--io-text-muted`). It is the only reusable label primitive that uses correct HTML semantics in any of the three modules, and would require minimal adaptation to become a cross-module shared component.
249	
250	7. **Destructive confirmation pattern — Settings `ConfirmDialog` usage.** Settings uses `shared/components/ConfirmDialog` for destructive actions across most pages (`Certificates`, `Email`, `BulkUpdate`, `AuthProviders`, `PointManagement`). Console implements three different inline confirmation dialogs; Designer implements per-dialog confirm flows with no shared primitive. Settings demonstrates that a shared confirmation dialog is sufficient for the full range of destructive actions in a complex module.
251

272	7. **Cat 7, Deviations, Shared column** — Changed "All three: none use `--io-input-*` tokens" to accurate per-module split (Console/Settings: none; Designer ShapePointSelector: two). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #3 / Cat 7 table rows 4 and 8.
273	
274	8. **Cat 8, Deviations, Settings** — Replaced "`--io-info` undefined" with confirmation that `--io-info: #3b82f6` is defined at index.css:72; fallback equals token value, no visual regression. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #5 (AuthProviders.tsx:528).
275	
276	9. **Cat 10, Notes, Designer** — Replaced "`io-multiselect-active` has no observable CSS rule" with confirmation that rule exists at index.css:904-909 (drop-shadow filter on selected nodes during multiselect). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #6.
277	
278	10. **Cat 11, Visual Properties, Settings** — Changed `--io-modal-backdrop` label from "(undefined)" to "(defined: `var(--io-surface-overlay)` at index.css:139)". Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #8.
279	
280	11. **Cat 11, Deviations, Settings** — Removed false "undefined" claim for `--io-modal-backdrop`; noted it is defined and used in ≥6 Settings files; corrected `window.confirm()` count from 4 to 8 with file breakdown (OpcSources:3, Import:3, CameraStreams:1, SupplementalConnectorsTab:1). Source: 03-verification.md discrepancies #7 and #8.
281	
282	12. **Cat 11, Notes, Shared column** — Changed "All three: `--io-modal-backdrop` and `--io-modal-bg` tokens are not used" to accurately note `--io-modal-backdrop` IS used in ≥6 Settings files; only `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` are unused across all three. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #8 / List 1 Item 11 correction.
283	
284	13. **Cat 11, Implementation, Settings** — Updated `window.confirm()` count in implementation row from "4" to "8". Source: 03-verification.md discrepancy #7.
285	
286	14. **List 1, Item 7** — Changed "All three modules ignore the five `--io-input-*` tokens" to partial-use statement (Console/Settings: none; Designer ShapePointSelector: two). Source: 03-verification.md List 1 Item 7 correction.
287	
288	15. **List 1, Item 11** — Changed "All three ignore `--io-modal-bg`, `--io-modal-backdrop`, and `--io-modal-radius`" to accurate statement: only `--io-modal-bg` and `--io-modal-radius` unused across all three. Source: 03-verification.md List 1 Item 11 correction.
289	
290	16. **List 2, Item 7, Settings undefined tokens** — Removed four tokens from Settings undefined list that ARE defined: `--io-modal-backdrop` (discrepancy #8, index.css:139), `--io-info` (discrepancy #9, index.css:72), `--io-space-*` (discrepancy #10, index.css:148-164), `--io-surface` (discrepancy #11, index.css:29). Retained genuinely undefined: `--io-text-on-accent`, `--io-overlay`, `--io-accent-rgb`. Source: 03-verification.md discrepancies #8–#11.
291	
292	### Additional Discrepancies — 03c-pre-reconciliation.md, Section 3
293	
294	17. **03c Discrepancy A — `--io-surface-sunken`**: Token confirmed registered at index.css:26. Reviewed 02-comparison.md — no hedged language about `--io-surface-sunken` was present in the comparison file; it was already treated as a valid token. No text change to 02. The underlying inconsistency finding (Cat 5, Deviations, Designer: equipment tiles use `--io-surface-sunken` vs `--io-surface-elevated` for other tiles) remains a valid real inconsistency in surface-tier choice. Source: 03c Section 3, Discrepancy A.
295	
296	18. **03c Discrepancy B — `--io-accent-subtle`**: Token confirmed registered at index.css:42 (`rgba(45,212,191,0.1)`). Reviewed 02-comparison.md — `--io-accent-subtle` does not appear in the comparison file; hedged claims were only in 01-designer.md. No text change to 02. The CanvasLayerRow indigo fallback (`rgba(99,102,241,0.1)`) is dead code with a wrong hue — latent but not breaking. Source: 03c Section 3, Discrepancy B.
297	
298	19. **03c Discrepancy C — `--io-border-subtle`**: Token confirmed registered at index.css:46 (`#27272a`). Reviewed 02-comparison.md — `--io-border-subtle` appears in Cat 1 Visual Properties (Settings) already treated as a valid token reference. No hedged language present. No text change to 02. Source: 03c Section 3, Discrepancy C.
299	
300	### Shared Infrastructure Additions — 03c Section 2 + 03-verification.md Missing-from-Comparison
301	
302	20. **Category 5 Shared Infrastructure — selection.css + MarqueeLayer.tsx (full entry)**: Added five-field entry covering the `var(--accent)` prefix bug (undefined token; should be `var(--io-accent)`), invisible selection overlay, and wrong hardcoded marquee background color (`rgba(80,180,255,0.08)` vs `var(--io-accent-subtle)`). Sources: 03-verification.md missing-from-comparison #1; 03c Sections 2.1 and 2.2.
303	
304	21. **Category 8 Shared Infrastructure — alarmFlash.css (full entry)**: Added five-field entry covering hardcoded alarm hex colors (values match dark-theme tokens but do not adapt to light/HPHMI themes). Sources: 03-verification.md missing-from-comparison #2; 03c Section 2.3.

307	
308	23. **Category 10 Shared Infrastructure — selection.css + MarqueeLayer.tsx (secondary entry)**: Added abbreviated cross-reference entry in Cat 10 (primary entry is Cat 5). Sources: same as #20; 03c category assignment: Cat 10 secondary.
309	
310	24. **Category 10 Shared Infrastructure — alarmFlash.css (secondary entry)**: Added abbreviated cross-reference entry in Cat 10 (primary entry is Cat 8). Sources: same as #21; 03c category assignment: Cat 10 secondary.
311	
312	25. **Category 10 Shared Infrastructure — operationalState.css (secondary entry)**: Added abbreviated cross-reference entry in Cat 10 (primary entry is Cat 8). Sources: same as #22; 03c category assignment: Cat 10 primary, Cat 8 secondary.
313	
314	26. **Category 10 Shared Infrastructure — lod.css (full entry)**: Added five-field entry for structural-only CSS with no deviations; recorded for completeness per instruction. Sources: 03-verification.md missing-from-comparison #4; 03c Section 2.5 (category assignment: Cat 10 exclusively).
315

317	
318	27. **Per-module audit files not updated**: 01-console.md, 01-designer.md, and 01-settings.md retain their original (some incorrect) content. 02-comparison.md is the authoritative source post-reconciliation. All corrections in this log apply to 02-comparison.md only.
